text: CAS safety argument cites wrong mechanism (SQLite WAL + IMMEDIATE) — Munin doesn't use BEGIN IMMEDIATE. Safety comes from single-process sync writes.
Remote Ollama dispatch via OLLAMA_LAPTOP_URL already covers the primary use case. Worker mode adds complexity for marginal benefit in v1.
Head-of-line blocking: limit:1 query + affinity filter means workers skip tasks and wait 30s. Not a 20-line fix.
Worker: field leaks machine topology into task API, collides with Ollama-host: axis.
Heartbeat collision: two workers writing tasks/_heartbeat/status would overwrite each other.
Stale task recovery is startup-only, not periodic. Draft incorrectly claimed Pi would catch stuck laptop tasks.
No framework (LangChain/CrewAI) needed for this problem. LangGraph only if durable multi-step workflows emerge later.
Hardcoded Pi paths more extensive than draft acknowledges: HOME=/home/magnus in 4+ locations, grimnir-bot git identity.
Phase 1 should start with observability (surface invocation journal metrics), not a blind laptop-first default flip.
Phase 2 must include Git identity/push portability, not just path cleanup.
Phase 3 gate should be operational need, not hardware arrival.
